template.name: TS_Initialiser

location.label: To initialize the train parameters in the Trackside system after communication is established.

template.name: TS_CommunicationManager

location.name: WaitForTPR
location.label: clkCM_MuteTimer <= MUTE_EXPIRED_TIMEOUT && clkCM_SessionTimer <= SESSION_EXPIRED_TIMEOUT

This automaton represents a comm medium from OB to TS. The comm relies on two clocks: a mute timer and a session timer. If no TPR or VTD is received before the mute timer expires, the comm is considered down. It can be restored if both TPR and VTD are received before the session timer expires, after verifying that the ID and train length match the last known train. During downtime, no data exchange occurs between the OB and TS.

location.name: WaitForReconnection
location.label: clkCM_SessionTimer <= SESSION_EXPIRED_TIMEOUT

template.name: TS_TrainManager_Main

location.name: Waiting
location.label: The Train Manager is responsible for processing TPR messages. Each TPR includes the SFE and CRE of each train, which can be forwarded to the Track Status Manager. Previously, the TPR only contained the SFE and L_TRAININT. This concept should be improved in future work so that the Track Status Manager receives not only the SFE and CRE, but also the track segments that are occupied, released, or unknown.

template.name: TS_TrainManager_ReqTPR

location.label: clkTM_ReqTPR <= REQ_TPR_TIMEOUT
Amenable to simplifications for the verification phase. This abstracts concrete conditions to require a TPR.

template.name: TS_TrainManager_AckVTD

location.label: To send an acknowledgment after receiving VTDReceivedF before the timeout.
clkTM_AckVTD <= ACK_TD_TIMEOUT

template.name: TS_TrainManager_IntChk

location.label: clkTM_IntegrityTimer <= INTEGRITY_WAIT_TIMEOUT
To check that the Train Manager receives integrity-confirmed TPR data before the timeout. The clock is reset once an integrity-confirmed TPR is received.

template.name: TS_TrackStatusManager_Global

location.label: To check if the point is occupied or not (PERFORMINGRAIL D2.2, p. 86).

template.name: TS_TrackStatusManager_Local
template.parameter: const int i, const int start_loc_int

location.name: Occupied
location.label: To set a track segment as occupied or unknown. Further development is needed so that the Track Status Manager stores only the unknown, released, and occupied segments sent by the Train Manager.

template.name: TS_RouteManager
TS_ReserveManager

location.label: To reserve the track segment: if the track is free, it will be reserved until the end of the route. If not, it will be reserved only up to the CRE of the leading train. It also checks if a point is reserved by another train when requested by the Route Manager. (PERFORMINGRAIL D2.2, p. 88)

template.name: TS_PointManager
template.parameter: const int i, const int m

location.name: idle
location.label: To check the point when the RM requests to reserve a point: 1st, checks if the point is occupied (retval1). 2nd, checks if the point is reserved (retval2). If both are clear, the point can be moved to the desired position. After, it verifies whether the point condition is normal (continueFlag). (PERFORMINGRAIL D2.2 p 102)

template.name: TS_PointManager_local
template.parameter: const int i, const int m
template.declaration: int PointID = -1;

location.name: Entrypoint
location.label: To model the movement of the point to the desired position according to the RM’s request, after the check from the PM global automaton.

template.name: TS_MAManagement

location.name: ComputeMA
location.label: This automaton is inspired by ASTRAIL

template.name: Ex_TrafficManagementSystem

location.label: To model the TMS, which periodically requests MAs for three trains, it is considered a centralized controller. It can also be modeled as distributed to make it more generic.
LOC_AbsTime<=RouteSetTime

template.name: Ext_TIMS

location.name: initTIMS
location.label: t_tims <= TIMS_sending_II_TIMEOUT
This automaton emulates the behaviour of the TIMS block.

template.name: Ext_TrainLocalizationUnit

location.name: Wait_Location_Request
location.label: This automaton emulates the behavior of Train Localization Unit. It regularly receives from the train position reporting automaton requests for location. It computes the location and sends it back to the Train position reporting automaton. For this automaton, we only represent the exchanged signals.

template.name: Ext_TrainDataManagement
template.parameter: int ID_Train, int category, int32_t Length , int32_t max_speed

location.name: Wait
location.label: This automaton represents a part of the behavior of train which is related to train data management. Train data are required to compute integrity status. Train data automaton receives a request to send train data, fills train data structure and sends trainData signal to the function SpeedDistanceSupervision.

template.name: Ext_TrainMovement_MAIN
template.parameter: const int id, const int start_loc_int, int ID_LRBG_init, const int32_t destinationTrain, const int32_t start_speed_int, const int32_t average_speed_int

location.name: I_GO
location.label: c<=freqLU
This automaton models the train dynamics; it moves based on the given Movement Authority (MA).

template.name: Ext_PositionUpdate

location.label: c<=sensorDelay

location.name: Step
location.label: LOC_C_freq<=LOC_freq && LOC_AbsTime' == 0.01
This automaton updates the train every 0.1 seconds. The system discretized at this interval.

template.name: InitOnBoard
template.parameter: int ID_Train, bool initValueTrainData, bool initValueACKTrainData, bool initValueLastLostTrainData, bool initValueLastConfirmedTrainData

location.label: This automaton initializes some Onboard parameters.

template.name: OB_IIMUpdating
template.declaration: clock t_II ; const int INTEGRITY_CHECK_TIMEOUT = 2*10;//supossed to be fast 2*10 ; //unit 0,1 sTime of sending Integrity info to TPR from IIM

location.name: Init
location.label: t_II <= INTEGRITY_CHECK_TIMEOUT
This automaton sends each INTEGRITY_CHECK_TIMEOUT the current integrity status to the TPR_Management automaton. The value sent of integrity is stored in the variable LOC_sentII.

template.name: OB_IIMStatusManagement

location.name: Integrity_Confirmed_By_TIMS
location.label: This model corresponds to PERFORMINGRAIL D2.3 for the LTI use case, excluding driver behavior.

template.name: OB_SpeedDistanceSupervision

location.label: We took the initial conditions defined in Table 7.21 page 138 in D2.2 (Valid Train data are always available, Valid Train Data have been acknowledged by the RBC, Train is moving in the middle of the track). This automaton requests train data from trainDataManegement in case that train is standstill and no valid train data available. Then, it receives from TrainData automaton the signal about Train data, sends these information to the Trains_Management function and waits for acknowledgement from this latter. In the case that the train is moving, train data can not be requested subset 26, part 5, page 74.

location.name: wait_TD_ACK
location.label: t_wait <= wait_TD_ACK_TIMEOUT

transition.label: LOC_Available_Train_Data [ID_Train] == false && TrainSpeedSentToTPR[ID_Train] != 0
LOC_Ack_Train_Data_RBC [ID_Train] = false, LOC_Available_Train_Data_Last_Lost [ID_Train] = false, LOC_Available_Train_Data_Last_Confirmed [ID_Train] = false

template.name: OB_TPRManagement
template.parameter: const int ID_Train, const int start_loc_int

location.name: Idle
location.label: t_tpr <= T_CYCLOC

This automaton emulates the behavior of Train Position Reporting function. It receives the current integrity status from IIM updating automaton, receives a request for Position Report (RequestPositionReport) from Train Management, sends the train position report which includes, mainly, the train position and the train integrity information to Train Management.

query.comment: Please dont forget to change the exploration mode to concrete
Please dont forget to change the exploration mode to concrete
margin 5 (66/309 runs) Pr(<> …) in [0.169217,0.263553] (95% CI) Unreachable with randomnized

plot.title: Probability Density Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=2.2204e-16, bucket count=1 Runs: 309 in total, 66 (21.359%) displayed, 243 (78.641%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [530, 530] Mean estimate of displayed sample: ≈ 530
